task_completion.description: Page 1 displays a title containing 'Marine' and either 'Food Web' or 'Ecosystem'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Title text visible on the first page of the flipchart.

task_completion.description: Page 2 shows at least 7 of the 8 required organisms (Phytoplankton, Kelp, Zooplankton, Krill, Sea Urchin, Herring, Sea Otter, Orca) as distinct labeled shapes arranged in approximately 3 vertical trophic levels (producers at bottom, consumers in middle, top predators at top).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple distinct labeled shapes at different vertical positions on page 2, with producer organisms lower and consumer/predator organisms higher.

task_completion.description: Page 2 has at least 10 arrows or lines connecting organisms to show feeding relationships, forming a dense web-like network pattern (not a simple chain).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple lines or arrows connecting shapes on page 2. The connections should form a web pattern with multiple crossing paths, not just a single chain.

task_completion.description: Page 3 shows an energy pyramid with 3 stacked rectangles decreasing in width from bottom to top. The rectangles should be labeled with trophic level names (Producers, Primary Consumers, Top Predators or similar) and include text about energy decreasing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three horizontally-stacked rectangular shapes decreasing in size from bottom to top, with text labels for each level and an indication of energy flow.

task_completion.description: Page 4 shows a carbon cycle diagram with at least 5 labeled stages connected by arrows in a circular or loop arrangement. Stages should include terms like Atmosphere, Photosynthesis, Decomposition, and at least two others.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Shapes arranged in a circular or loop pattern connected by arrows on page 4, with text labels for each stage of the carbon cycle.

task_completion.description: The flipchart file is saved as marine_ecosystem.flipchart in the Flipcharts directory with at least 4 pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog completed showing marine_ecosystem as the filename, or the ActivInspire title bar showing the saved file name, or a file listing confirming the file exists.

integrity.description: The agent used the ActivInspire GUI application to create the flipchart content.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the ActivInspire interface (toolbar, canvas, page browser) being actively used throughout the task.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to programmatically generate the flipchart file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing Python scripts, echo commands, or direct file manipulation to create .flipchart content.

integrity.description: Content was genuinely drawn and typed on the ActivInspire canvas using drawing and text tools, not pasted as a pre-made image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Individual shapes, text boxes, and lines are visible as separate canvas objects, showing active use of ActivInspire drawing tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file is a 4-page flipchart saved as 'marine_ecosystem.flipchart' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Flipcharts\'. Page 1 is a title page. Page 2 must contain a food web with 8 organisms (Phytoplankton, Kelp, Zooplankton, Krill, Sea Urchin, Herring, Sea Otter, Orca) in 3 trophic levels connected by at least 12 arrows. Page 3 must have an energy pyramid with 3 labeled trophic levels (Producers, Primary Consumers, Top Predators). Page 4 must have a carbon cycle with 6 stages (Atmosphere, Photosynthesis, Marine Plants, Marine Animals, Decomposition, Ocean Sediments) connected in a loop by arrows.
